questions.q: Explain the importance of proper disposal and recycling of electronic waste. Discuss the environmental and health impacts of e-waste if not disposed of properly.
questions.answer: E-waste disposal and recycling are crucial for mitigating environmental and health risks. Improper disposal can lead to pollutants entering the soil, water, and air, affecting ecosystems and human health. Recycling e-waste reduces the demand for primary materials, conserves natural resources, and minimizes greenhouse gas emissions.
marking_scheme: Importance of proper e-waste disposal (1 mark)
Environmental impacts of improper e-waste disposal (1 mark)
Health impacts of improper e-waste disposal (1 mark)

questions.q: What are the different types of e-waste? Describe the common materials found in each type.
questions.answer: E-waste is categorized into several types, including: household appliances, computers and peripherals, consumer electronics, and telecommunication devices. Household appliances contain materials like copper, steel, and aluminum, while computers and peripherals contain copper, plastics, and rare earth metals.
marking_scheme: Materials found in household appliances (1 mark)
Materials found in computers and peripherals (1 mark)
Materials found in consumer electronics (1 mark)

questions.q: Discuss the role of governments and organizations in e-waste management. What initiatives have been taken by these entities to promote sustainable e-waste practices?
questions.answer: Governments and organizations play a crucial role in e-waste management through policies, regulations, and awareness campaigns. Initiatives include e-waste collection facilities, recycling programs, and Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) schemes.
marking_scheme: Role of governments in e-waste management (1 mark)
Role of organizations in e-waste management (1 mark)

questions.q: A consumer electronics manufacturer generates 100 kg of e-waste per day. If the e-waste is recycled, the recycling process has a 90% efficiency rate, meaning 90 kg of the e-waste is recycled, and 10 kg is sent to landfills.
questions.answer:
Calculation:
 1. Total e-waste generated per day: 100 kg
 2. E-waste recycled: 90% of 100 kg = 0.9 x 100 = 90 kg
 3. E-waste sent to landfills: 10 kg
 Therefore, the manufacturer recycles 90 kg of e-waste and sends 10 kg to landfills.

questions.q: A company collects 500 kg of e-waste per month from households. If the e-waste is processed at a rate of 80 kg per day, how many days will it take to process the collected e-waste?
questions.answer:
Calculation:
 1. Total e-waste collected per month: 500 kg
 2. E-waste processed per day: 80 kg
 3. Number of days to process e-waste: 500 kg / 80 kg/day = 6.25 days
 Therefore, it will take the company 6.25 days to process the collected e-waste.
marking_scheme: Number of days to process e-waste (2 marks)

questions.q: A recycling facility receives 200 kg of e-waste containing 30% copper, 20% aluminum, and 50% plastics. If the facility can process 80 kg of e-waste per day, how many days will it take to process the received e-waste?
questions.answer:
Calculation:
 1. Total e-waste received: 200 kg
 2. Copper content: 30% of 200 kg = 0.3 x 200 = 60 kg
 3. Aluminum content: 20% of 200 kg = 0.2 x 200 = 40 kg
 4. Plastics content: 50% of 200 kg = 0.5 x 200 = 100 kg
 5. Total valuable materials: 60 kg + 40 kg + 100 kg = 200 kg
 6. Number of days to process e-waste: 200 kg / 80 kg/day = 2.5 days
 Therefore, it will take the facility 2.5 days to process the received e-waste.
marking_scheme: Number of days to process e-waste (2 marks)
